name: org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter
description: Ghostwriter is a Java command-line documentation and project automation engine from the Machai project. It scans project layouts, discovers source, test, documentation, and module folders, extracts embedded @guidance directives from Java, Markdown, HTML/XML, PlantUML, Python, TypeScript, and dedicated @guidance.txt files, and sends normalized prompt fragments to a configured GenAI provider. The artifact also supports Act workflows: reusable TOML-defined prompt templates with instructions, inputs, inheritance, prologue and epilogue acts, episode sequencing, repeat and move-to-episode control, custom act directories, file-pattern scanning, exclusions, non-recursive mode, and multi-threaded module processing. Ghostwriter exposes host-integrated function tools for reading, writing, patching, and listing files, executing allow-listed commands with deny-list checks and bounded log capture, retrieving web content and REST API responses, managing project-scoped context variables, processing guidance-tagged files asynchronously, and controlling task or process lifecycle. It is useful for AI-assisted documentation generation, repository review, codebase modernization, automated prompt-driven workflows, project-aware agent tooling, and safe host-side automation for Java-based applications.

usageContext: Use as a CLI entry point for scanning a project and processing embedded @guidance tags with a configured GenAI provider.
Use as a reusable Java library for building file-system processors, reviewer implementations, act processors, project context tools, web tools, file tools, and command execution tools.
Use in multi-episode AI workflows where an act may move to another episode, repeat the current episode, end a task, or terminate execution.
Use in host-controlled AI agents that need safe project-relative file access, bounded command logs, REST calls, web content retrieval, and project-scoped context variables.

constructors.description: Creates an Act processor for the supplied project root directory, GenAI provider or model identifier, and configuration source. The processor loads TOML act definitions, applies act instructions and prompt inputs, scans matching files or folders, and executes prompt episodes against the configured provider.

examples.description: Creates an ActProcessor, points it at a custom acts directory, selects an act with an additional prompt, and scans Java source files. In production, provide a real org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator implementation instead of null.

constructors.description: Constructs a GuidanceProcessor that scans a project tree for embedded @guidance directives, uses reviewer implementations to format prompt fragments, and dispatches the result to the configured GenAI provider. It supports single-module and multi-module layouts and processes modules child-first.

examples.description: Creates a processor for Java guidance tags, scans the current project, and reads the processing report. Provide a configured GenAI model and Configurator in a real application.

constructors.description: Creates a command deny-list checker and loads operating-system-specific deny-list rules. Use this together with an allow-list before executing host-side commands.

examples.description: Initializes the command security checker and verifies that a command does not match deny-list rules before execution.

features.name: Ghostwriter main
features.signature: public static void main(java.lang.String[] args) throws java.io.IOException, org.apache.commons.cli.ParseException
features.description: Starts the Ghostwriter command-line application. The CLI parses options, loads configuration, selects guidance or act processing mode, and scans the configured project paths.

examples.snippet: org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter.main(new java.lang.String[] { "--help" });
examples.description: Invokes the Ghostwriter CLI entry point programmatically. The real command-line arguments depend on the host distribution and configuration.

features.name: setAct
features.signature: public void setAct(java.lang.String act) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Loads an act definition and applies it as the current execution default. The argument supports the form <name> [prompt], where the optional prompt is injected into the act inputs template. Acts are TOML files loaded from built-in resources or a custom directory and may use inheritance via basedOn.

examples.description: Selects the mvn/bindex act and supplies additional prompt text that is inserted into the act template.

features.name: scanDocuments
features.signature: public void scanDocuments(java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String path) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Configures scanning based on a directory, glob pattern, or regex pattern and starts scanning the project folder. Matching files are processed by the active processor.

examples.description: Scans the current project for Java and Markdown files. Use a supported glob or regex pattern appropriate for the Java PathMatcher implementation.

features.name: perform
features.signature: public java.lang.String perform(java.io.File projectDir, java.io.File file) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Reviewer SPI method that analyzes a file and returns a formatted prompt fragment when actionable @guidance content is present. Implementations handle Java, Markdown, HTML/XML, PlantUML, Python, TypeScript, and @guidance.txt files according to each format's comment syntax.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer reviewer = new org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.JavaReviewer();
java.lang.String promptFragment = reviewer.perform(new java.io.File("."), new java.io.File("src/main/java/com/example/App.java"));
examples.description: Runs the Java reviewer against a Java source file. The result is null when the source file does not contain an @guidance tag.

features.name: executeCommand
features.signature: public java.lang.Object executeCommand(java.lang.String command, java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.String> properties, java.lang.String dir, int tailResultSize, java.lang.String charsetName, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Executes an explicitly allowed command within a project-relative working directory, applies security checks, captures bounded output, and persists command logs for later retrieval and searching.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.CommandFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.CommandFunctionTools();
java.lang.Object result = tools.executeCommand("mvn test", new java.util.HashMap<java.lang.String, java.lang.String>(), ".", 4096, "UTF-8", new java.io.File("."), null);
examples.description: Runs Maven tests from the project root when the command is allowed by host configuration. The returned object contains command status and output information as defined by the runtime.

features.name: getWebContent
features.signature: public java.lang.String getWebContent(java.lang.String url, java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.String> headers, int timeout, java.lang.String charsetName, boolean textOnly, java.lang.String selector, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator)
features.description: Fetches HTTP or HTTPS content with optional headers, timeout, charset, CSS selector extraction, plain-text extraction, and basic authentication via URL userInfo. File URLs may also be read relative to the project context.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.WebFunctionTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.WebFunctionTools();
java.lang.String text = tools.getWebContent("https://example.com", null, 5000, "UTF-8", true, "body", new java.io.File("."), null);
examples.description: Retrieves the text content of the body element from a web page using a five-second timeout.

features.name: putProjectContextVariable
features.signature: public static java.lang.String putProjectContextVariable(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String value, java.io.File projectDir)
features.description: Sets or updates a named variable in project-scoped context so that acts, episodes, and tool calls can share state across workflow steps.

examples.snippet: java.lang.String message = org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.ProjectContextFunctionTools.putProjectContextVariable("releaseNotes", "Draft completed", new java.io.File("."));
examples.description: Stores a project context variable that can be read by a later act episode or tool call.

features.name: applyPatch
features.signature: public static void applyPatch(java.lang.String filePath, java.util.List<java.lang.String> patchLines, java.nio.charset.Charset charset) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Applies a unified diff patch to a text file using the supplied character set. This is used by project file tools to update only specified sections of a file.

examples.snippet:
java.util.List<java.lang.String> patchLines = java.util.Arrays.asList("--- a/example.txt", "+++ b/example.txt", "@@ -1 +1 @@", "-old", "+new");
org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.PatchApplier.applyPatch("example.txt", patchLines, java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
examples.description: Applies a small unified diff to example.txt. The patch must be valid and must match the current file content.

customizations.name: Reviewer
customizations.description: Implement the Reviewer SPI to add support for another file format or a custom guidance extraction rule. A reviewer declares supported file extensions and returns a formatted prompt fragment or null for each candidate file.

examples.description: Defines a custom reviewer implementation. Register it with the host's SPI or processor integration so Ghostwriter can discover it.

customizations.name: Act TOML definitions
customizations.description: Create or override TOML act files in a custom acts directory. Supported keys include instructions, inputs, gw.threads, gw.excludes, gw.nonRecursive, prologue, epilogue, and basedOn. Unknown keys are forwarded to the underlying configuration.

examples.snippet:
# acts/custom-review.toml
instructions = "Review the project files and produce concise documentation."
inputs = "Analyze this file: %s"
gw.excludes = "target,.git"
gw.nonRecursive = "false"
examples.description: Creates a custom act file that can be selected with ActProcessor.setAct("custom-review").

customizations.name: AbstractFileProcessor
customizations.description: Subclass AbstractFileProcessor or AIFileProcessor to reuse project traversal, include matching, exclusion handling, module recursion, and concurrency behavior while providing custom per-file processing logic.

examples.description: Shows how to implement a custom AI-backed file processor while reusing Ghostwriter traversal and prompt execution.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.gw.reviewer.Reviewer
studs.description: Service-provider interface for components that scan project files and extract @guidance instructions for downstream processing. Implementations are format-specific and return normalized prompt fragments with project-relative context.

examples.description: Uses the Reviewer interface polymorphically with the Markdown implementation.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.AbstractFileProcessor
studs.description: Base implementation for processors that traverse a project directory and perform work on files and folders. It centralizes scanning, path filtering, exclusions, module recursion, concurrency, and delegation to processFile.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor processor = new org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor(new java.io.File("."), "openai:gpt-4.1", null);
processor.setExcludes(new java.lang.String[] { "glob:target/**", "glob:.git/**" });
examples.description: Uses an AbstractFileProcessor subclass and configures project-relative exclude rules.

examples.title: Install Ghostwriter as a Maven dependency
examples.scenario: Use Ghostwriter APIs from a Java application or test utility.
steps: Add the Maven dependency for org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT to your pom.xml.
Ensure the Machai snapshot or internal repository that hosts the artifact is available to Maven.
Instantiate GuidanceProcessor or ActProcessor with a project directory, a GenAI provider identifier, and a Configurator.
Call scanDocuments with a project-relative directory, glob pattern, or regex pattern.
examples.snippet:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.machanism.machai</groupId>
  <artifactId>ghostwriter</artifactId>
  <version>1.2.2-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>
examples.description: Adds Ghostwriter to a Maven project so its processors, reviewer implementations, and function tools can be used directly.

examples.title: Configure and run guidance-tag processing
examples.scenario: Generate AI-assisted documentation from embedded @guidance comments in a repository.
steps: Configure the GenAI provider and model in the host Configurator or Ghostwriter properties file.
Add @guidance comments to Java, Markdown, HTML/XML, PlantUML, Python, TypeScript, or @guidance.txt files.
Create a GuidanceProcessor with the project root and model identifier.
Scan the desired project files with scanDocuments.
Review the processing report returned by getReport.
examples.description: Runs guidance extraction against Java files and dispatches generated prompts to a configured GenAI model.

examples.title: Configure and run an Act workflow
examples.scenario: Execute a reusable TOML-defined workflow against a project path.
steps: Create an acts directory in the project or select a built-in act from the classpath.
Write a TOML act containing instructions and inputs; optionally configure gw.excludes, gw.threads, gw.nonRecursive, prologue, epilogue, or basedOn.
Create an ActProcessor with the project root and model identifier.
Call setActsLocation to point at custom acts if needed.
Call setAct with the act name and optional prompt text.
Call scanDocuments with the target path or pattern.
examples.description: Runs a custom act against Java source files and collects the generated provider outputs.

examples.title: Install and invoke the ready-to-use CLI JAR
examples.scenario: Use Ghostwriter as a command-line documentation tool in a local repository or CI job.
steps: Build or obtain the Ghostwriter distribution JAR. The Maven pack profile assembles a gw.jar with org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.Ghostwriter as the main class.
Configure provider credentials, model selection, project directory, excludes, and scan path in the host Ghostwriter properties file or via supported CLI options.
Place custom act TOML files in the configured acts location if act mode is required.
Run java -jar target/gw.jar with the desired options from the repository root.
Inspect generated output, reports, or modified files according to the selected act or guidance workflow.
examples.snippet:
mvn -Ppack install
java -jar target/gw.jar --help
java -jar target/gw.jar --projectDir . --path "glob:src/main/java/**/*.java" --model "openai:gpt-4.1"
examples.description: Demonstrates installation by building the packaged JAR, basic configuration through command-line options, and real-world invocation against Java source files. Exact option names may vary with the host distribution; use --help to inspect supported CLI flags.

examples.title: Use host-integrated file, web, command, and context tools
examples.scenario: Expose controlled project automation capabilities to a GenAI-backed workflow.
steps: Register Ghostwriter function tool classes with the GenAI provider in the host application.
Use FileFunctionTools for project-relative read, write, patch, and directory listing operations.
Use CommandFunctionTools for allow-listed commands with deny-list checking and persisted logs.
Use WebFunctionTools for HTTP retrieval and REST calls with optional headers and timeouts.
Use ProjectContextFunctionTools to pass state between act episodes.
examples.description: Shows practical use of project-relative file access and project-scoped context state in a host-controlled workflow.
